name: Marker (PDF to Markdown)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-3.0); GPU recommended for speed; no API costs

performance.notes: 1-5 pages/sec on GPU; slower on CPU; much faster than LLM-based approaches

agent_readiness.notes: State-of-the-art PDF-to-Markdown converter using specialized OCR and layout detection models. Handles columns, tables, equations (LaTeX), code blocks, headers. Better than PyMuPDF/pypdf for complex documents. GPU-accelerated. Table extraction with structure preservation. Used for RAG document pipelines where high-quality text extraction matters. Also processes EPUB, Word, HTML.

gotchas: GPL-3.0 license — incompatible with commercial closed-source products
GPU required for practical speed at scale
Scanned PDFs (image-only) slower due to OCR model overhead
Table extraction still imperfect for complex merged cells
best_when: RAG pipelines needing high-quality PDF text extraction
Converting research papers, reports, books to clean Markdown
Extracting tables from PDFs while preserving structure
Processing academic PDFs with equations and multi-column layouts
avoid_when: Simple PDFs where pypdf or pdfplumber suffices
summary: Marker is a state-of-the-art PDF-to-Markdown converter using specialized OCR and layout models. Handles multi-column PDFs, tables, equations (LaTeX), code blocks, and headers. GPU-accelerated. Better than PyMuPDF for complex documents. GPL-3.0 licensed. Best for RAG pipelines needing high-quality text extraction from research papers and technical documents.
